summary: The suite covers all four core requirements — preview mode, apply, conflict detection, and undo script generation — with sensible grouping into four test classes and a clean make_files helper fixture. An end-to-end test even executes the generated shell script under bash to verify round-trip correctness. However, rigor has meaningful gaps: there are no tests for invalid/malformed regex patterns, a non-existent directory, filenames containing spaces or special shell characters (the undo script quotes paths, but this is never exercised), the case where a regex matches but the substitution produces an identical name (identity no-op), or recursive-vs-flat directory behaviour. The bash integration test also lacks a skip marker for non-POSIX platforms. Minor design issues: ConflictError is imported inside two test methods rather than at the module level alongside the other imports, and no pytest.mark.parametrize is used where several near-identical scenarios could be collapsed. Overall a trustworthy suite that would catch most regressions, but edge-case gaps around invalid inputs and path-encoding scenarios leave some risk.
